# Emitted Crate

## Output modes

`gec` supports two output modes via `crategen::OutputMode`:

### Crate mode

Writes a complete Cargo-compatible crate directory:

```text
output_dir/
├── Cargo.toml
├── build.rs          (if link requirements exist and build_script enabled)
└── src/
    └── lib.rs
```

### Source bundle mode

Writes only the Rust source file (no `Cargo.toml` or `build.rs`):

```text
output_dir/
└── src/
    └── lib.rs
```

## Cargo.toml

The generated `Cargo.toml` includes:

```toml
[package]
name = "mylib-sys"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
# Auto-generated by gec <version>
```

## build.rs

When link requirements exist, `gec` generates a `build.rs` that emits
Cargo link directives:

```rust
fn main() {
    println!("cargo:rustc-link-lib=mylib");
    println!("cargo:rustc-link-search=native=/usr/lib");
}
```

## Overwrite policies

`crategen::OverwritePolicy` controls behavior when the output directory
already exists:

| Policy | Behavior |
|---|---|
| `Fail` | Return an error if the directory is not empty |
| `Clean` | Remove existing contents before writing |
| `Overwrite` | Write over existing files without removing extras |

## Crate naming

`crategen::normalize_crate_name()` ensures valid Cargo package names:

- Replaces non-alphanumeric characters (except `_`) with `_`
- Rejects empty names
- Rejects names starting with a digit

## Usage

```rust
use gec::crategen::{emit_crate, OutputMode, OverwritePolicy};

let emitted = emit_crate(
    &output.projection,
    &config,
    "/tmp/mylib_sys",
    OutputMode::Crate,
    OverwritePolicy::Clean,
).unwrap();

assert!(emitted.cargo_toml.is_some());
assert!(emitted.lib_rs.exists());
```
